title: Tail-recursive tree walker with pattern-matched base cases
prompt: Write lib/my_app/tree/walker.ex (module MyApp.Tree.Walker). It walks a binary tree represented as {:leaf, value} | {:node, left, right, value} and returns a list of values in-order. Implement walk/1 using ONLY multi-clause function heads — no case, no cond, no if. Base case: walk({:leaf, v}) returns [v]. Internal case: walk({:node, left, right, v}) returns walk(left) ++ [v] ++ walk(right). Also implement walk/2 with an accumulator for tail-recursion: walk(tree, acc). Its two heads handle :leaf (append to acc) and :node (recurse right first, then v, then left reversed — since tail-recursion builds backward). Finally implement in_order/1 that calls walk(tree, []) and reverses the result.
tier_rationale: Requires understanding tail-recursion semantics + the 'build backward, reverse at end' idiom + dispatching via function heads on tagged tuples. Sonnet typically writes a non-tail-recursive version or falls back to Enum.reduce that misses the tree structure.
